source_metadata.title_raw: Yolanda M. Summerhill - A-18-113 - July 23, 2018 - Fullerton

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

July 23, 2018

Yolanda M. Summerhill
Senior Deputy City? Attorney
Jones & Mayer, PLC
3777 N. Harbor Blvd.
Fullerton, CA 92835

Re:     Your Request for Advice
        Our File No. A-18-113

Dear Ms. Summerhill:

        This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of interest or Section 1090.

        Also, note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) I FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for additional advice.

QUESTION

        May Jones & Mayer legally advise the elections official/city clerk of the City of La Habra in a recall effort brought by residents against La Habra Mayor Tim Shaw?

CONCLUSION

        Yes. Jones & Mayer does not have a potentially disqualifying interest in the recall effort, and may elections official/city clerk of the City.

FACTS AS PROVIDED BY REQUESTOR

        Jones & Mayer serves as contract city attorney to the City of La Habra and the City Attorney, Richard D. Jones, is also a resident of the City. A planning application has been filed with the City Planning Department that would convert a private golf course to a residential

_______________________
¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. A-18-113
Page No. 2

development project ("Project"). The City Council and City staff are represented by outside
legal counsel on this Project due to its proximity to Mr. Jones' residence in accordance with the
Political Reform Act. At a recent City Council meeting, Mayor Tim Shaw was presented
with recall papers from a group of residents that oppose the Project. The City Council has not
approved and/or denied the Project, as it is currently undergoing environmental review under the
California Environmental Quality Act.

The City Attorney's Office seeks advice as to whether Jones & Mayer is prohibited from
providing legal advice to the elections official/city clerk on the recall effort being brought against
Mayor Shaw given the fact that Jones & Mayer and Mr. Jones have recused themselves from
advising the City on the Project.

ANALYSIS

Conflict of Interest Law

Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or using
his or her position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a financial interest.
An official has a financial interest in a decision if the decision will have a reasonably foreseeable
and material effect on those interests enumerated in Section 87103. Interests from which a conflict
of interest may arise are:

1. Any business entity in which the public official has a direct or indirect investment worth
$2,000 or more. (Section 87103(a).)

2. Any real property in which the public official has a direct or indirect interest worth
$2,000 or more. (Section 87103(b).)

3. Any source of income, except gifts or loans by a commercial lending institution made in
the regular course of business on terms available to the public without regard to official
status, aggregating $500 or more in value provided or promised to, received by, the
public official within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is made. (Section
87103(c).)

4. Any business entity in which the public official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management. (Section 87103(d).)

5. Any donor of, or any intermediary or agent for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating
[$470] or more in value provided to, received by, or promised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is made. (Section 87103(e).)

6. Personal finances, meaning the financial effect of a governmental decision on the
personal finances of a public official or his or her immediate family.

File No. A-18-113
Page No. 3

You note that Jones & Mayer serves as contract city attorney for the City. You also not that Jones & Mayer and Mr. Jones have recused themselves from advising the City on the Project. However, you have provided no facts to suggest either Jones & Mayer or Mr. Jones has an interest that may be foreseeably affected by the recall effort. Accordingly, Jones & Mayer is not prohibited from providing legal advice to the elections official/city clerk on the recall effort.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Brian G. Lau
Acting General Counsel

By: Zachary W. Norton
Senior Counsel, Legal Division

ZWN:jgl
